bead.title: bead-lifecycle: emit rule fingerprint and operator-override signal in intake.blocked
bead.description:
PROBLEM
intake.blocked entries appended by readiness carry only outcome and detail strings. There is no stable rule fingerprint, so duplicate warnings stack on every readiness pass, and there is no operator_override field, so operators cannot see that readiness honored a prior acceptance instead of downgrading the bead.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 constructs the intake.blocked entry without a rule_fingerprint (stable hash of rule_id + bead_id + relevant evidence keys) and without an operator_override boolean.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the intake.blocked entry schema to include rule_fingerprint and operator_override. Compute rule_fingerprint as a stable hash over rule_id, bead_id, and the evidence keys that drove the rule. Set operator_override=true when the sibling guard determined the bead was operator-promoted and the downgrade was skipped. Use rule_fingerprint to dedup entries so an identical rerun does not append a duplicate. Surface both fields in `ddx bead show` output for the bead's intake.blocked list.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new readiness rules or changing existing rule evaluation logic.
- Changing the readiness terminal flow itself (covered by sibling implementation bead).
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text (covered by sibling spec bead).
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: this epic. Depends on the implementation bead for the operator-promotion detection function it consumes to set operator_override.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestIntakeBlockedCarriesRuleFingerprint asserts every intake.blocked entry emitted by readiness has a non-empty rule_fingerprint stable across reruns with identical inputs.
2. TestIntakeBlockedSetsOperatorOverride asserts operator_override=true on entries written when readiness skipped a downgrade because the bead was operator-promoted, and false otherwise.
3. TestIntakeBlockedFingerprintDedupes asserts running readiness twice with identical inputs appends the entry only once.
4. TestBeadShowSurfacesFingerprintAndOverride asserts `ddx bead show` renders rule_fingerprint and operator_override for each intake.blocked entry.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passing.

Executable with reasonable interpretation, but clarifications would reduce rework.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}
